cards.back: A Datagram is a self-contained unit of data that is transmitted over a network, without guarantee of delivery or order of arrival

cards.front: What is DVR in Unicast Routing Protocols?
cards.back: Distance-Vector Routing (DVR) is a type of routing protocol that uses a distance-vector metric to determine the best path to a destination

cards.front: What is LSR in Unicast Routing Protocols?
cards.back: Link State Routing (LSR) is a type of routing protocol that uses a link-state metric to determine the best path to a destination

cards.front: What is PVR in Unicast Routing Protocols?
cards.back: Path-Vector Routing (PVR) is a type of routing protocol that uses a path-vector metric to determine the best path to a destination

cards.front: What is RIP in Unicast Routing Protocols?
cards.back: Routing Information Protocol (RIP) is a unicast routing protocol that uses a distance-vector metric to determine the best path to a destination
